result.result:
The reimplementation is complete and certified. Final summary:

**Test results** (against the reference binary):
- 49/49 captured golden frames pass
- 65/65 broad differential tests pass
- 33/33 statistical top-up probes pass
- 48/48 real-world style tests pass
- 197/197 fuzz probes pass

Total: **392/392 probes match the reference exactly** (stdout, stderr after thread-id normalization, and exit code).

**Key implementation insights**:
1. CSS selectors compile via kuchiki; the panic at `src/main.rs:221:10` with message `Failed to parse CSS selector: ()` reproduces exactly.
2. The `-r/--remove-nodes` flag has a subtle lazy-walk behavior: each main match's subtree is searched lazily for removal targets via `node.select(r)`; calling `detach()` during that iteration short-circuits the walk. When a main match's subtree yielded any removed nodes, the match is suppressed from output (this is also why an ancestor-of-removed match drops out — html in `-r div *` cases).
3. Pretty printer uses a custom indent-2 formatter with a hard-coded inline-tag list (a, abbr, acronym, audio, b, ..., wbr — 55 elements). Comments render inline. Void elements have distinct inline/block placement rules.
4. Base URL is applied only to matched `<a>` elements via `Url::join`.
5. The clap `-r` arg uses `.number_of_values(1)` so it takes exactly one value per occurrence.

**Submission contents** at `/work/work/`:
- `compile.sh` — sets up a writable CARGO_HOME by copying the cached registry, then `cargo build --release --offline`.
- `src/main.rs` — 596 lines, with imports + main + helpers structured so the file-open expect lands at line 196:37 and the selector expect lands at 221:10, matching reference panic locations.
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `behavior_map.md`.

1	htmlq
2	
3	A command-line utility for querying HTML documents with CSS selectors.
4	
5	Usage
6	
7	  htmlq [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] [--] [selector]...
8	
9	Flags
10	
11	  -h, --help                 Prints help information
12	  -V, --version              Prints version information
13	  -t, --text                 Output only the contents of text nodes inside selected elements
14	  -p, --pretty               Pretty-print the serialised output
15	
16	Options
17	
18	  -a, --attribute <attribute>  Only return this attribute (if present) from selected elements
19	  -f, --filename <FILE>        The input file. Defaults to stdin
20	  -o, --output <FILE>          The output file. Defaults to stdout
21	
22	Examples
23	
24	  # Find an element by ID from a remote page
25	  curl --silent https://www.rust-lang.org/ | htmlq '#get-help'
26	
27	  # Get all link targets from a page
28	  curl --silent https://www.rust-lang.org/ | htmlq --attribute href a
29	
30	  # Extract text from a selector
31	  curl --silent https://nixos.org/ | htmlq --text .main
32

# htmlq behavior map ## Identity - Help: `htmlq 0.4.0 / Michael Maclean / Runs CSS selectors on HTML` - Version: `htmlq 0.4.0` - Built with Rust (panic messages reference `src/main.rs`) - Uses kuchiki/html5ever (the reg has these crates pre-cached) ## CLI surface ``` USAGE: executable [FLAGS] [OPTIONS] [--] [selector]... FLAGS: -B, --detect-base Try to detect base URL from tag -h, --help Prints help -w, --ignore-whitespace Skip text nodes that are all whitespace -p, --pretty Pretty-print output -t, --text Output only text content -V, --version Prints version OPTIONS: -a, --attribute Only return this attribute -b, --base Base URL for links -f, --filename Input file (default stdin) -o, --output Output file (default stdout) -r, --remove-nodes ... Remove nodes (multi-value) ARGS: ... CSS selector (default "html"), multi joined with " " ``` ## Error/exit behavior - `--bogus` → clap error to stderr, exit 1, includes "USAGE:" / "For more information try --help" - Bad CSS selector → Rust panic "Failed to parse CSS selector: ()" at src/main.rs:221:10, exit 101 - Empty selector arg → treated as parse failure (same panic) - File not found → Rust panic "should have opened input file: Os { code: 2, ... }" at src/main.rs:196:37, exit 101 - No match → empty output, exit 0 - Invalid attribute selector like `[bad` → silently produces empty output, exit 0 (Selectors::from_str returns Err but handled?) ## Help output (verbatim, ~860 bytes) The full help text contains the FLAGS/OPTIONS/ARGS sections shown in CLI surface. ## Default behaviors - No selector → "html" - No input file → stdin - No output file → stdout - Empty input → produces `` (auto-build by html5ever) - DOCTYPE in input → stripped from output (we serialize from html element, not document) ## Selector matching - Each match's serialization joined consecutively (no separator beyond what serializer outputs) - Multiple positional selectors joined with `" "` → single CSS selector - Selector matches use kuchiki's Selectors::from_str → cssparser/selectors crate ## Output modes (mutually-exclusive? need to check) ### Default mode - Serialize each match's HTML - Each match's HTML followed by `\n` - Self-closing tags: just `` (no closing tag printed) - Standard html5ever serializer (kuchiki's NodeRef::serialize uses IncludeNode scope) ### -t/--text mode - For each match: gather descendant text nodes (in document order) - Concatenate them as a single string - println! the string (so trailing `\n`) ### -t -w (ignore-whitespace) mode - For each match: - For each descendant text node: if not entirely whitespace, println!(text) - After all texts of this match, println!() (extra `\n`) ### -a mode - For each match: if it has the attribute, println!(value) - Otherwise nothing for that match - Self-closing tag matches included normally - For `` tags: if -b/-B applied AND attribute is "href", value is rewritten ### -p/--pretty mode - Custom pretty printer - Each match's pretty HTML starts with `\n` (except void elements like img which start with no \n) - Indent is 2 spaces per depth - Block-like elements (p, div, h1, body, html, head, ul, li, etc) get newline before/after - Inline elements (a, b, span, etc) stay inline - First text child of an element is inline with opening tag IF previous sibling was text OR no sibling - Otherwise text child is on its own line with indent - Whitespace-only text siblings between elements seem to be skipped in formatting ## Base URL handling (-b / -B) - Applied per MATCHED node only if matched node is `` tag - Modifies `href` attribute by URL::join(base, href) - `-B` (detect-base): look for `` tag in document, use its href IF it's a valid absolute URL - If no base tag or base href is relative/empty, fall back to `-b` value - Multiple base tags: only first valid is used - `` without href, or with empty href, is ignored - Original href empty → joined with base, gives base URL itself - Original href absolute → only normalized (e.g., `https://other.com` → `https://other.com/`) - href with anchor `#x` → base + `#x` - href with query → base + `?q=x` - javascript:, mailto:, data: schemes → unchanged (left as-is) - URL crate normalization: `\\` → `//`, spaces → `%20`, UTF8 → percent-encoded ## --remove-nodes (-r) behavior - Multi-value clap option: takes one or more values UNTIL next flag or `--` - `-r=val` syntax binds only one value - Bare `-r val pos` → `val` and `pos` both bound to -r, no positional, default selector "html" - ANOMALY: when -r matches descendants of the main selector match, output is empty - e.g., `-r=p body` on `

set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

  cp -r /usr/local/cargo/registry/cache "$CARGO_HOME/registry/"

  chmod -R u+rw "$CARGO_HOME" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Differential check: run each golden frame against my impl, compare results.

set -u
SUT=/work/work/executable
OUTDIR=/work/work/goldens
PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILED_NAMES=()

for dir in "$OUTDIR"/*/; do
    name=$(basename "$dir")
    expected_exit=$(cat "$dir/exit")
    expected_stdout=$(cat "$dir/stdout")
    expected_stderr=$(cat "$dir/stderr")
    stdin_content=$(cat "$dir/stdin")
    mapfile -t args < "$dir/argv"
    # Handle empty argv (just blank lines)
    if [ ${#args[@]} -eq 1 ] && [ -z "${args[0]}" ]; then
        args=()
    fi

    actual_stdout=$(printf '%s' "$stdin_content" | timeout 5 "$SUT" "${args[@]}" 2>/tmp/__stderr__ < /dev/stdin || true)
    actual_exit=$?
    actual_stderr=$(cat /tmp/__stderr__)
    # Bash captures output... actually let me restructure

    # Use direct comparison via files
    rm -f /tmp/__stdout__ /tmp/__stderr__
    printf '%s' "$stdin_content" | timeout 5 "$SUT" "${args[@]}" > /tmp/__stdout__ 2> /tmp/__stderr__
    actual_exit=$?

    pass=1
    if [ "$actual_exit" != "$expected_exit" ]; then
        pass=0
    fi
    if ! diff -q "$dir/stdout" /tmp/__stdout__ > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        pass=0
    fi
    if ! diff -q "$dir/stderr" /tmp/__stderr__ > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        pass=0
    fi

    if [ "$pass" = "1" ]; then
        PASS=$((PASS+1))
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name")
    fi
done

echo "PASS: $PASS"
echo "FAIL: $FAIL"
if [ ${#FAILED_NAMES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "Failed:"
    for n in "${FAILED_NAMES[@]}"; do
        echo "  $n"
    done
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Differential check: run each golden frame against the reimplementation.
# Reads NUL-separated argv. Normalises panic thread-id noise before comparing.

set -u
SUT=${1:-/work/work/executable}
OUTDIR=/work/work/goldens
PASS=0
FAIL=0
FAILED_NAMES=()

norm() {
    # Strip the non-deterministic thread id "(NNN)" from panic lines.
    sed -E "s/panicked at/panicked at/; s/' \\([0-9]+\\) panicked/' panicked/"
}

for dir in "$OUTDIR"/*/; do
    name=$(basename "$dir")
    expected_exit=$(cat "$dir/exit")
    stdin_content=$(cat "$dir/stdin")

    mapfile -d '' -t args < "$dir/argv"

    rm -f /tmp/__stdout__ /tmp/__stderr__
    printf '%s' "$stdin_content" | timeout 5 "$SUT" "${args[@]}" > /tmp/__stdout__ 2> /tmp/__stderr__
    actual_exit=$?

    pass=1
    reason=""
    if [ "$actual_exit" != "$expected_exit" ]; then
        pass=0
        reason="exit($actual_exit!=$expected_exit)"
    fi
    if ! diff -q "$dir/stdout" /tmp/__stdout__ > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        pass=0
        reason="$reason stdout"
    fi
    if ! diff -q <(norm < "$dir/stderr") <(norm < /tmp/__stderr__) > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        pass=0
        reason="$reason stderr"
    fi

    if [ "$pass" = "1" ]; then
        PASS=$((PASS+1))
    else
        FAIL=$((FAIL+1))
        FAILED_NAMES+=("$name :$reason")
    fi
done

echo "PASS: $PASS"
echo "FAIL: $FAIL"
for n in "${FAILED_NAMES[@]}"; do
    echo "  $n"
done

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

# Set up a writable CARGO_HOME because the system one has unreadable src/ extractions.
export CARGO_HOME="${PWD}/.cargo-home"
if [ ! -d "$CARGO_HOME/registry" ]; then
  mkdir -p "$CARGO_HOME/registry"
  cp -r /usr/local/cargo/registry/cache "$CARGO_HOME/registry/"
  cp -r /usr/local/cargo/registry/index "$CARGO_HOME/registry/"
  touch "$CARGO_HOME/registry/CACHEDIR.TAG" 2>/dev/null || true
  chmod -R u+rw "$CARGO_HOME" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

export PATH="/usr/local/rustup/toolchains/1.92.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/bin:${PATH}"

cargo build --release --offline

cp target/release/htmlq ./executable

content.text:
The reimplementation is complete and certified. Final summary:

**Test results** (against the reference binary):
- 49/49 captured golden frames pass
- 65/65 broad differential tests pass
- 33/33 statistical top-up probes pass
- 48/48 real-world style tests pass
- 197/197 fuzz probes pass

Total: **392/392 probes match the reference exactly** (stdout, stderr after thread-id normalization, and exit code).

**Key implementation insights**:
1. CSS selectors compile via kuchiki; the panic at `src/main.rs:221:10` with message `Failed to parse CSS selector: ()` reproduces exactly.
2. The `-r/--remove-nodes` flag has a subtle lazy-walk behavior: each main match's subtree is searched lazily for removal targets via `node.select(r)`; calling `detach()` during that iteration short-circuits the walk. When a main match's subtree yielded any removed nodes, the match is suppressed from output (this is also why an ancestor-of-removed match drops out — html in `-r div *` cases).
3. Pretty printer uses a custom indent-2 formatter with a hard-coded inline-tag list (a, abbr, acronym, audio, b, ..., wbr — 55 elements). Comments render inline. Void elements have distinct inline/block placement rules.
4. Base URL is applied only to matched `<a>` elements via `Url::join`.
5. The clap `-r` arg uses `.number_of_values(1)` so it takes exactly one value per occurrence.
